nodes.description: The local daemon for the Open Design application. It serves the chat UI, proxies messages to AI agents, manages projects and persistence, and exposes APIs for media generation and design systems.
Metadata describing the application version, including version number, release channel, packaging status, platform, and architecture.
A set of design tokens (colors, typography, spacing) and conventions defined in a DESIGN.md file within a design-system registry.
The capability to generate image, video, and audio assets using various AI models and providers, invoked via the 'od media generate' command.
A service provider that offers media generation models (e.g., OpenAI, Volcengine, xAI Grok, HyperFrames, MiniMax, FishAudio).
A specific AI model used for media generation (e.g., gpt-image-2, seedance-2.0, suno-v5).
A background process that manages the runtime environment for the daemon and provides IPC communication.
A SQLite database ('app.sqlite') used for persisting projects, conversations, messages, tabs, and deployments.
A workspace container in Open Design that holds files, conversations, and configuration for a specific design task.
A chat thread within a project where the user interacts with an AI agent.
An individual interaction (user or assistant) within a conversation, potentially containing text, tool calls, and artifact references.
A record of a project or file being deployed to an external hosting provider like Vercel.
An open file tab within a project workspace.
A user-authored comment anchored to a specific element in an artifact preview.
Design principles and patterns (e.g., typography, color) loaded from markdown files to guide AI generation.
Implementation of the Pi JSON-RPC protocol over stdio for communication with Pi-based agents.
Handler for parsing GitHub Copilot CLI's JSONL output stream into daemon-friendly UI events.
Handler for parsing Claude Code's streaming JSON output into daemon-friendly UI events.
Component responsible for preparing and executing deployments of HTML artifacts to Vercel.
A companion pet character from the Codex hatchery, featuring a spritesheet and metadata.
A specialized AI capability defined by a SKILL.md file, specifying triggers, modes, and design system requirements.
An anti-slop linter that checks generated HTML for common AI design tropes and adherence to craft rules.
Manages the lifecycle of agent executions (runs) and handles event streaming via SSE.
A generic handler for mapping JSON-based event streams from various agents (OpenCode, Gemini, Cursor) to UI events.
Agent Control Protocol, a JSON-RPC based communication standard used for controlling AI agents.
Generates a token-based preview page for a design system from its DESIGN.md file.
Generates a high-fidelity product showcase page to demonstrate a design system in a realistic context.
Routes media generation requests to the appropriate provider and manages the execution flow, including stub fallbacks.
Generates text-based previews for documents (PDF), spreadsheets (XLSX), and presentations (PPTX).
A metadata structure that describes a generated artifact, its kind, renderer, and supporting files.

Runtime support for previewing React components via srcdoc.

nodes.description: A web app that turns natural-language briefs into editable, previewable design artifacts by orchestrating existing code agent CLIs.

Web on Vercel + daemon on user's machine via tunnel.
Web on Vercel + direct Anthropic API (no daemon, degraded experience).

Next.js 16 web app (apps/web) providing the UI, preview renderer, and chat interface.

Generates a single editable screen (HTML/JSX).
Generates multi-slide HTML presentations.

Generates and refines DESIGN.md files.
